title: State transition
description: A transition resolves an ambiguous observation toward one hidden state on cited evidence, or records a FORBIDDEN transition learned from a regression. Every successful authoring/repair becomes a reusable transition pattern; every wrong-gloss regression becomes a forbidden transition. Transitions are the audit trail of the language state machine.

kind.description: resolve=observation->single hidden state; split=observation->multiple states (quarantine); repair=entry field corrected; forbidden=a collapse that must never be made

to.description: the hidden state(s) reached (one for resolve/repair; many for split); for forbidden, the WRONG target that must be rejected

evidence.description: evidence rungs that licensed the transition (internal-only references are NOT public)

procedure.description: sarf/nahw procedure that owns this transition

why.description: for forbidden transitions: the collision class and the regression it prevents
